exact_statement.name: R1a trivial 2-face boundary long-chord exclusion
exact_statement.formal_surface: For every repository-native admissible finite-patch FGL instance I, every trivial 2-face tau in Phi_2^triv(I), and every long-chord edge e_i with i in {1,2}, e_i is not in supp(partial tau).
exact_statement.r1_dependency_role: R1a supplies the generator-level exclusion input needed before R1b can assert closure under F2-linear combinations.

weakest_missing_input.name: TrivialTwoFaceBoundaryLongChordAbsence
weakest_missing_input.logical_form: For every repository-native admissible finite-patch FGL instance I, trivial 2-face tau in Phi_2^triv(I), and long-chord edge e_i, TrivialTwoFace(I,tau) and LongChordEdge(I,e_i) imply e_i notin supp(partial tau).
weakest_missing_input.why_weakest: This is exactly the R1a generator-level statement. It does not assert R1b linear-combination closure, R1c maximal-separation geometry, or the combined R1 Long-Chord Exclusion Lemma.

combination_boundary.after_this_surface: R1a remains a statement surface until the generator-level absence lemma is proved in the repository-native FGL model.
combination_boundary.r1b_required_after_r1a: Linear combinations of trivial face boundaries cannot create either long chord by F2 cancellation.
combination_boundary.r1c_required_after_r1b: Maximal-separation geometry forbids any trivial witness boundary from traversing a diameter-spanning long chord.
combination_boundary.r1_combination_required: R1 requires R1a, R1b, and R1c; this artifact supplies none of those proofs.

required_non_claims: does not prove Long-Chord Exclusion Lemma
does not prove P vs NP
does not prove any Clay problem
